---
title: "changing which partition is mounted as / in feisty"
date: 2007-05-01
forum: General Help
---

### Post by jakihairi on 2007-05-01
How do I change which partition is mounted as / ? I copied partition /dev/hdb3 (which is currently mounted as root) to /dev/hdb1, and I want to make ubuntu mount  /dev/hdb1 as / .  I tried editing fstab, but ntfs-config clobbers it on start up. Also, do i have to make a change to /boot/grub/menu.lst ?(I assume so)
Here is fstab:
```
...
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
...
# Entry for /dev/hdb3 :
UUID=b6f03c3f-5a8c-4927-a33e-54f2e8f6943f / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
...
/dev/hdb1 /media/windows ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 1
```

/dev/hdb1 was windows, and I was mounting it with ntfs-3g, but then i deleted /dev/hdb1 . 

/boot/grub/menu.lst:

```

title		Ubuntu, kernel 2.6.20-15-generic
root		(hd0,5)
kernel		/vmlinuz-2.6.20-15-generic root=UUID=b6f03c3f-5a8c-4927-a33e-54f2e8f6943f ro quiet splash
initrd		/initrd.img-2.6.20-15-generic
quiet
savedefault
```

The most disturbing part is that vol_id gives me the same UUID for /dev/hdb1 and /dev/hdb3 !!! I don't understand the switch to UUIDs, they're unreadable. :confused:  

Ubuntu's great (replaces windows completely after this :) ), thanks in advance. 
PS: How do I prevent ubuntu from overwriting configuration files in general?

---

### Post by gazj on 2007-05-01
I am not certain about this, but i think the UUID is something to do with the drives information being encrypted, so as you can not drop it into another machine and take the data from it.  I could be wrong.  The fstab will need changing,  although i think i am a little stuck with the new UUID

Yes i believe grub will need to be changed, the hd(0,5) bit.  i can't understand how /dev/hdb3 has ended up as 0,5 it would normally be 1,2 though, and also what is /dev/hda in your system ???

---

I have an article laid out about partition mounting in Ubuntu.  It explains how to modify the fstab file and all that good stuff.

### Post by jakihairi on 2007-05-01
Let me be more specific, I have two problems:

1)  ntfs-config  is overwriting the /etc/fstab so I can't make changes to it
2) even if i could change it, /dev/hdb1 and /dev/hdb1 have the same UUID using vol_id

Let me clarify, I have no ntfs partitions on this hard drive any more, but do have an external with ntfs. 

Running the ntfs-config gui definitely overwrites /etc/fstab so i assume the overwriting occurs either at shutdown or reboot, and since ntfs-config has no options or help, i have no idea how to change this behavior.

I've been looking, but I can't find anything about preventing ubuntu from overwriting my config files in general, which is very annoying since it seems to just clobber them whenever it feels like it (sometimes it overwrites, sometimes it doesn't).

Thanks again.

---

### Post by jakihairi on 2007-05-01
I just fixed it! ntfs-config was the problem, everything worked once i removed ntfs-config. If you understand how fstab works there's no need for ntfs-config (apparently).  I'm steering clear of these UUIDs as well.

---

### Post by gazj on 2007-05-01
can you not remove the ntfs-3g packages with synaptic, surely this will stop your fstab getting overwritten

---
